name: HyPhy
description: Hypothesis Testing using Phylogenies. Open-source software package for the analysis of genetic sequences using techniques in phylogenetics, molecular evolution, and machine learning.
longDescription: HyPhy is our flagship software package. It features a rich scripting language for limitless customization of analyses, with over 10,000 registered users and over 3,000 citations in peer-reviewed publications. It's the engine behind many of our web-based tools.

name: Datamonkey
description: Run state-of-the-art statistical models through the comfort of your browser.
longDescription: High-performance computing web-server for analyzing evolutionary signatures in sequence data. Datamonkey has processed over 500,000 analyses and received over 1,000 citations. We are currently transitioning to datamonkey3 for even better performance.

name: HIV-TRACE
description: Identify Transmission Clusters for molecular epidemiology of HIV-1 and other pathogens.
longDescription: Currently in use by the CDC and many other public health and research entities for monitoring HIV transmission clusters. It's a critical tool for real-time molecular surveillance.

name: phylotree.js
description: JavaScript library for application development and interactive data visualization in phylogenetics.
longDescription: A tool suitable for building JavaScript applications where users can view and interact with phylogenetic trees. It extends d3.js and is designed to be highly modular and extensible.

name: tn93
description: High-speed C++ implementation for calculating TN93 genetic distances.
